decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Since material-only tuning has stalled, I will implement a coordinated geometry and material change: increase the heat spreader width to cover more of the domain laterally and simultaneously increase its conductivity to maximize lateral spreading efficiency.

changes.reason: Widening the spreader to the maximum allowed bound (1.0m) increases the cross-sectional area for lateral heat flow, reducing thermal resistance from the chip to the side boundaries.
Increasing conductivity to the upper bound enhances the spreader's ability to distribute heat laterally across the newly widened geometry, working synergistically with the width increase.

expected_effects: Significant reduction in chip peak temperature due to lower lateral thermal resistance.
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer.
risk_notes: Both changes hit their respective upper bounds; if this step fails to satisfy constraints, further improvements may require modifying the base plate or changing the problem formulation.
Geometric change ensures the spreader spans the full width, which is physically valid within the 1.2m domain but leaves 0.1m margins on each side.
